free_text: Prompt emission of gamma-rays and neutrons from the
fast neutron induced fission of 232Th at neutron
energies near and above the fission threshold
Homi Bhabha National Institute, Mumbai
Folded Tandem Ion Accelerator (FOTIA)
Proton beam with energies 3.5, 4.1 and 4.8 MeV
were bombarded on a natural Li metallic foil target of
thickness ~4.0 mg/cm2.
A twin section fission trigger detector
covering a solid angle of nearly 4pi was used to
detect the fission fragments. Within each section are
a cathode and an anode composed of copper plate G-10
discs with 7.5 cm dia., and separated by 2.0 mm thick
Teflon spacer rings.
Two 1.5 inch X 1.5 inch cylindrical CeBr3
detectors, were placed 20 and 22 cm distance from the
fission target, and at angles of 33 and 58 degrees with
respect to the direction of the proton beam.
Two EJ301 liquid scintillation detectors
(12.7 cm in dia. and 5.0 cm thick) were positioned at
75 cm and 82.5 cm from fission target, respectively,
and at angles of 55 and 80 degrees with respect to
the direction of the proton beam.
The detector efficiency and response function were
determined by GEANT4.
No information on the source of uncertainty
Data received from R.G.Thomas
SAMRUDHI R KANJARPANE
